summary: This podcast episode description outlines Martin Heidegger's motivation for writing Being and Time. It highlights his rejection of unexamined modern assumptions about existence and his drive to recover forgotten truths by revisiting historical philosophical texts.

principles.statement: Modern answers to fundamental questions of existence are built on unexamined assumptions.
principles.rationale: Over time, deep truths about what it means to be in the world have been obscured or forgotten, leading to flawed modern assumptions.
principles.source_quote: Heidegger came to believe that many of the modern answers to these questions were based on old, unexamined assumptions.

principles.statement: We must return to the great philosophical texts of the past to recover obscured truths.
principles.rationale: By revisiting historical texts, we can bypass unexamined modern assumptions and reveal deep truths about existence that have been lost.
principles.source_quote: Instead of accepting those assumptions, Heidegger wanted to return to the great philosophical texts of the past and see if he could recover and reveal deep truths that had been obscured or forgotten.

anti_patterns.description: Accepting modern answers to expansive questions (like "Why are we here?") without questioning their foundations.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: These answers are often based on old, unexamined assumptions that obscure deeper truths about existence.
